title: equisetum fluviatile var. uliginosum
intro: Equisetum fluviatile var. uliginosum, commonly known as marsh horsetail, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Equisetaceae family and is native to wetland areas in Europe, Asia, and North America. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and silica, which possess medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Equisetum fluviatile var. uliginosum include reducing swelling and pain, treating kidney stones, and alleviating symptoms of urinary tract infections, as well as providing antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Herbal preparations of marsh horsetail include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used externally to treat skin conditions and wounds, and internally to support urinary and kidney health.
